title: Improved thresholds and SSA accuracy can reduce most satellite collision risk
summary: A new study suggests that enhancing space situational awareness (SSA) accuracy and tightening conjunction thresholds can significantly reduce satellite collision risks in low Earth orbit (LEO). The findings highlight the importance of improved data precision in mitigating orbital hazards.

title: Space Sovereignty Is No Longer Optional
summary: Space is now a critical infrastructure supporting modern life, including financial transactions and military operations. The article emphasizes the growing importance of space sovereignty as a necessity.

title: April 30: Opportunities for On-Orbit Computing
summary: An event titled 'Opportunities for On-Orbit Computing' is scheduled for April 30 in Washington, D.C., focusing on orbital data centers. The event is part of a series discussed in SpaceNews.

title: Tracking the next SDA challenge
summary: The U.S. government faces challenges in monitoring the crowded and contested space environment due to rapid commercial innovation in sensors and AI-driven insights. The Space Development Agency (SDA) is tasked with addressing these monitoring challenges.

title: Preparing for the next NASA budget battle
summary: Congress passed a fiscal year 2026 appropriations bill in January, rejecting proposed steep cuts to NASA's budget, including a 25% overall reduction and nearly 50% cut to science funding. The space community welcomed the decision, but future budget battles are expected.

title: A lunar base or a lunar economy?
summary: NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman's vision for a lunar base is praised, but the lack of a long-term economic development plan for the moon is criticized as a missed opportunity.

title: LeoLabs debuts space monitoring tool for military users
summary: LeoLabs has launched a new space monitoring tool called 'Delta' that goes beyond collision warnings to detect potential adversarial activities. This tool is specifically designed for military users.

title: Industry navigates NASA’s start-and-stop approach to commercial space stations
summary: NASA's plans to accelerate the replacement of the International Space Station with commercial alternatives have slowed down despite initial signals of acceleration. The industry is adapting to NASA's inconsistent approach to the Commercial Low Earth Orbit Destinations (CLD) program.

title: Artemis 2 science gets underway as Orion begins its return trip
summary: Scientists have started analyzing the data and images collected by the Artemis 2 crew during their lunar flyby. The Orion spacecraft has begun its journey back to Earth.

title: Capella Space wins $49 million contract for military communications satellite demo
summary: Capella Space secured a $49 million contract from the Space Development Agency to demonstrate a military communications satellite by 2027. This deal highlights Capella's role in advancing space-based military communications technology.

title: Large Hadron Collider gives scientists their best look yet at conditions right after the Big Bang
summary: The ALICE experiment at CERN's Large Hadron Collider has provided scientists with unprecedented insights into quark-gluon plasma, the primordial matter present moments after the Big Bang. This breakthrough offers a deeper understanding of the early universe's conditions.

title: 'The Expanse: Osiris Reborn' has lots of 'Mass Effect' DNA, but its developers are teasing a far more grounded sci-fi RPG
summary: 'The Expanse: Osiris Reborn' draws heavily from 'Mass Effect' while developing its own hard sci-fi identity. Owlcat Games is teasing a more grounded sci-fi RPG experience.

title: 'Audible screams of delight' from NASA scientists over micrometeorite impacts on the moon witnessed by Artemis 2 astronauts
summary: NASA scientists are excited about micrometeorite impacts on the moon observed by Artemis 2 astronauts during their flyby. These observations provide valuable data for future lunar missions.

title: Artemis 2 astronauts spy the Milky Way | Space photo of the day for April 8, 2026
summary: The Artemis 2 astronauts captured a stunning space photo of the Milky Way's galactic plane on April 8, 2026. The image highlights the dense concentration of stars in our galaxy.

title: Artemis 2 moon astronauts capture space history — on their iPhones
summary: The Artemis 2 astronauts are using iPhones to capture high-quality photos of the moon and Earth during their mission. This highlights the integration of consumer technology in space exploration.

title: Galaxy starves its supermassive black hole, loses 95% of its brightness
summary: A galaxy has experienced a dramatic 95% drop in brightness due to its supermassive black hole starving. This sudden change highlights the volatile nature of active galactic nuclei.

title: 'It just made it even more special': Being so far from Earth makes you appreciate our planet even more, Artemis 2 astronaut says
summary: Artemis 2 astronaut Christina Koch highlights the profound impact of seeing Earth from beyond the moon, emphasizing the 'overview effect' and a deeper appreciation for our planet. The astronaut reflects on how this perspective changes one's view of Earth.

title: The Artemis 2 space toilet is actually working fine. But there is another problem
summary: The space toilet for Artemis 2 is functioning properly, but engineers are addressing an issue with emptying the waste tank.

title: 'Project Hail Mary' sound designers used surprising animal sounds to create Rocky's musical alien voice (interview)
summary: The sound designers for 'Project Hail Mary' used unique animal sounds to create the musical alien voice of Rocky. They aimed to capture Rocky's 'sonic soul' through these unconventional sounds.

title: Relive Artemis 2's epic moon flyby with these amazing photos
summary: NASA's Artemis 2 astronauts shared detailed images from their historic moon flyby mission. The photos provide a stunning visual record of their journey around the moon.

title: Twin NASA Control Rooms Support Artemis Safety, Success
summary: NASA's Marshall Space Flight Center operates two control rooms supporting the Artemis II mission in lunar orbit, ensuring astronaut safety and mission success ahead of the crew's return to Earth on April 10.

title: Solar Eclipse of the Heart
summary: Artemis II astronauts aboard the Orion spacecraft captured a unique solar eclipse of the Moon during a lunar flyby on April 6, 2026. This rare observation was only possible due to their specific position in space.

title: NASA’s Artemis II Crew Beams Official Moon Flyby Photos to Earth
summary: NASA's Artemis II crew shared the first flyby images of the Moon, revealing previously unseen regions and a rare in-space solar eclipse. The photos, released on Tuesday, were updated on April 8, 2026, to reflect ongoing scientific observations.

title: Earthset
summary: The Artemis II crew captured images of Earth setting from lunar orbit on April 6, 2026, documenting the Moon's surface features such as craters, lava flows, and geological formations.

title: Experience the Rollout of SLS Hardware for Artemis III
summary: NASA invites digital creators and social media users to witness the rollout of the SLS core stage for Artemis III from Michoud Assembly Facility to Kennedy Space Center. This event highlights NASA's ongoing preparations for the Artemis III mission.

title: NASA to Host Media Call with Artemis II Crew on Way Home from Moon
summary: NASA will host a media call with the Artemis II crew on April 8, 2026, at 10:45 p.m. EDT as they return from their historic mission around the Moon. The 20-minute event will allow journalists to speak with the crew during their journey back to Earth.

title: SpaceX continues refinements at Starbase Pad 2 ahead of Booster 19 return
summary: SpaceX is making final preparations at Starbase Pad 2 for the return of Booster 19. The refinements are part of ongoing operations at the facility.

title: Artemis II breaks record, conducts lunar flyby
summary: Artemis II achieved a record-breaking lunar flyby with the Orion spacecraft, carrying astronauts Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover, Christina Koch, and Jeremy Hansen. This mission marks a significant milestone in NASA's Artemis program.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon 9 to loft Cygnus to ISS, Minotaur IV to launch from California
summary: A Falcon 9 rocket is set to launch a Cygnus spacecraft to the International Space Station, while a Minotaur IV rocket is scheduled to launch from California. This follows the halfway point of NASA's Artemis II mission.

title: Orion Spacecraft Races Toward Historic Lunar Flyby in Artemis II Mission
summary: NASA's Artemis II mission is in its critical mid-flight phase as the Orion spacecraft approaches a historic lunar flyby. This marks a significant milestone for the mission, which aims to test key systems ahead of future lunar landings.

title: Artemis II Orion performs TLI Burn — Now headed to the Moon
summary: NASA's Orion spacecraft successfully performed the Trans-Lunar Injection (TLI) burn, marking a historic milestone as it heads toward the Moon. This achievement is part of the Artemis II mission, the first crewed lunar mission in over 50 years.

title: Artemis II returns humanity to the Moon with launch on Wednesday evening
summary: The Artemis II mission is set to return humanity to the Moon with a launch scheduled for Wednesday evening. This follows the historic Apollo 17 mission, which concluded on Dec. 16, 1972.

title: Launch Preview: SLS, Falcon 9, Atlas V, and Soyuz launches comprise busy launch manifest
summary: Nine launches are scheduled this week, featuring rockets like SLS, Falcon 9, Atlas V, and Soyuz lifting off from Florida, California, and Russia.

title: America’s Rocket Factory comes Full Circle with Artemis
summary: America's Rocket Factory is highlighted for its role in NASA's Artemis program, marking a significant milestone in the return of crewed launches beyond low Earth orbit. The news underscores the company's contribution to the mission's preparations.

title: SpaceX delivers 119 payloads to Sun-synchronous orbit on Transporter 16
summary: SpaceX successfully launched 119 payloads to a Sun-synchronous orbit as part of the Transporter 16 mission from California. This marks another milestone in SpaceX's rideshare program.

title: NASA outlines Moon Base plans, pivots on Gateway
summary: NASA revealed detailed plans for a Moon base during its 'Ignition' event, marking a significant shift in its lunar exploration strategy. The agency is pivoting away from its previous Gateway-focused approach.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates 50 years of achievements with a commemorative booklet highlighting 50 hallmark accomplishments. The publication marks five decades of contributions by the European Space Agency.

title: Graphene and lasers for space propulsion
summary: An international research team demonstrated light propulsion using graphene aerogels in microgravity during ESA's parabolic flight campaign in May 2025. The study, published in Advanced Science, showed that lasers could efficiently propel these ultralight materials, offering potential for future satellite and solar sail applications.

title: Graphene and lasers for space propulsion
summary: Lasers and graphene could revolutionize space propulsion by steering solar sails and adjusting satellite positions. An experiment on a gravity rollercoaster demonstrated the potential of this innovative material.

title: A silicon chip that can do it all
summary: A breakthrough silicon chip has been developed that integrates multiple functions typically handled by separate components. This innovation could revolutionize space electronics by reducing size, weight, and power consumption for spacecraft.

title: Proba-3’s Coronagraph is alive!
summary: The Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft has re-established contact with ground control after a month-long communication loss due to an anomaly. The mission team reports that the spacecraft is now operational.

title: Upcoming ESA–Industry events on CM25 implementation
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) is hosting events to present the follow-up to the Council Meeting at Ministerial Level 2025 (CM25), detailing planned activities and industrial opportunities. These events aim to facilitate direct exchange between ESA and the industrial community.

title: Plato readies for space-like tests
summary: ESA's Plato mission, designed to discover Earth-like exoplanets, is undergoing critical space-like tests in the Large Space Simulator chamber. The spacecraft, equipped with 26 ultrasensitive cameras, is expected to launch on an Ariane 6 rocket in January 2027.

title: Former NASA astronaut Jeanette Epps visits ESTEC
summary: Former NASA astronaut Jeanette Epps visited ESTEC, the European Space Agency's technical center. This visit highlights international collaboration in space exploration.

title: ESA rolls out new costing software for its suppliers
summary: ESA will release ECOS version 6 on March 30, 2026, mandating its use for financial proposals in ESA Tender Actions by all supplier companies.

title: The State of ISAM 2026
summary: The article discusses the growing complexity of In-Space Servicing, Assembly, and Manufacturing (ISAM) by 2026, noting that it can no longer be treated as a single concept. The post is published by Payload.

title: Czech Radiation Chips on Artemis II Bring ISS Experience
summary: Czech-developed radiation chips will be part of the Artemis II mission, leveraging ISS experience to monitor solar flares during solar maximum. HERA will act as an early warning system for potential solar events.

title: Advocates Ready For NASA Science Funding Fight, Part II
summary: The Trump administration proposed an $18.8 billion NASA budget with significant cuts to science and STEM missions. Advocates and lawmakers are opposing these reductions, vowing to fight against the proposed cuts.

title: Starfish Closes $100M+ Series B
summary: Starfish Space has secured over $100 million in a Series B funding round led by Point72 Ventures, with Activate Capital and Shield Capital co-leading the investment.

title: The State of EO 2026
summary: The Earth Observation (EO) industry is experiencing growth as more organizations seek to deploy their own satellite capabilities. The State of EO 2026 report highlights this trend and its benefits.

title: How Earthlings Got Views of the Lunar Fly By
summary: Redwire developed 11 cameras installed inside and outside the Orion capsule, enabling live views of the lunar flyby for Earth viewers. The cameras were part of the Artemis I mission's payload.

title: The State of Satcom 2026
summary: SpaceX and Amazon are disrupting the satcom market, forcing traditional players and startups to pivot and find innovative ways to compete.

title: Artemis II Supplier Series: Orion’s Windows
summary: The article discusses the importance of Orion's windows for astronauts on the Artemis II mission, highlighting the significance of seeing space firsthand after years of training. It is part of a series covering suppliers for the Artemis II mission.

title: White House Requests $18.8B FY27 Budget for NASA
summary: The White House has proposed a $18.8 billion budget for NASA in fiscal year 2027, maintaining the same funding level as the 2026 request. The proposal includes cuts to NASA's science and STEM missions, similar to the previous year's budget.

title: Insurance is Commercial Space Nuclear’s Biggest Headache
summary: Insurance is identified as the biggest challenge for commercial space nuclear missions, with experts calling it a 'show stopper' during an industry seminar. The article highlights the significant hurdles faced by this emerging sector.

title: Live in the Booth: Sky Perfect JSAT President & CEO Eiichi Yonekura
summary: Sky Perfect JSAT, a major global satellite operator, is focusing on a multi-faceted strategy to expand its business in the coming years. Eiichi Yonekura, the company's President & CEO, discussed these plans in a recent interview.

title: FCC to Vote on Order to Update EPFD Framework
summary: The FCC is set to vote on an order to update satellite spectrum-sharing rules between GEO and NGSO systems. The proposed changes aim to replace the existing EPFD framework.

title: Italy and Argentina to Enhance Space Collaboration With Telespazio and VENG Deal
summary: Italy and Argentina are strengthening their space collaboration through a new multi-year deal between e-GEOS (a joint venture of the Italian Space Agency and Telespazio) and VENG. The agreement focuses on enhancing space data cooperation.

title: Northrop Grumman to Build HUGEO Satellite for Hungary’s 4iG
summary: Northrop Grumman has signed a deal with Hungary's 4iG Space and Defense Technologies to build the nation's first communications satellite, named HUGEO. The agreement was announced on Tuesday.

title: April/May Magazine: Space as Critical Infrastructure
summary: Via Satellite's April/May magazine focuses on space as critical infrastructure and cybersecurity challenges for lunar activities. It also covers Europe's increased investment in space defense.

title: It’s Unanimous: Space Already Functions as Critical Infrastructure
summary: Experts agree that space functions as critical infrastructure in the U.S., even though it is not officially designated as the 17th sector. The sector is deeply integrated into government, academia, and industry operations.

title: Cybersecurity in Space is Hard; In Cislunar Space, it’s Really Hard
summary: The article discusses the challenges of cybersecurity in space, particularly in cislunar space, highlighting the difficulties of applying terrestrial cybersecurity strategies like anomaly detection and software patching in space environments.

title: In-Space Mobility, Maturing From Last Miles to Superhighways
summary: NASA studied a reusable 'space tug' system as part of the Space Transportation System (STS) in the late 1960s. The concept is evolving into modern in-space mobility solutions, transitioning from short-range missions to broader applications.

title: Is Europe Moving Fast Enough to Close its Space Defense Gaps?
summary: Europe is accelerating efforts to address space defense gaps amid concerns over Russia's military buildup and potential threats to NATO within four years. The continent is racing to enhance its space capabilities to counter emerging security challenges.

title: Space Is the Best Offset Strategy
summary: The article discusses space as a key offset strategy in national security, leveraging technological advantages to counter adversaries' numerical or geographic strengths. It highlights the role of space in modern defense and deterrence.
